message:
test(fuzz): add differential errno-precedence fuzz target (bd-5cv2p)

Wire up a new structure-aware fuzz target that stress-tests the
errno-precedence contract of our Linux-only syscall wrappers against
host glibc. For each iteration it drives a small set of entrypoints
(renameat2, signalfd4, close_range, eventfd_write, fsconfig) through
overlapping invalid-input axes and asserts exact host-vs-ABI parity
on both success/failure and the exact errno returned.

- crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml: register fuzz_errno_precedence as
  a [[bin]] target next to the existing fuzz_open_syscalls entry so
  cargo-fuzz can build it.

- crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_errno_precedence.rs (651
  LOC): new target using libfuzzer_sys + arbitrary. Defines an opcode
  table (OP_RENAMEAT2 = 0, OP_SIGNALFD4 = 1, OP_CLOSE_RANGE = 2,
  OP_EVENTFD_WRITE = 3, OP_FSCONFIG = 4) plus a small pool of host
  FSCONFIG command codes, fabricates both valid and deliberately
  invalid file descriptors (HIGH_FD_BASE = 1_000_000 for guaranteed-
  closed fds), and then calls both the host glibc entrypoint and the
  ABI-layer wrapper with identical inputs so the harness can compare
  errno precedence byte-for-byte.

The bead (bd-5cv2p) calls out errno precedence as a primary
correctness surface for syscall wrappers, so this unblocks getting
that gate fuzzed continuously.

files.patch:
@@ -125,6 +125,11 @@ name = "fuzz_open_syscalls"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_open_syscalls.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_errno_precedence"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_errno_precedence.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_read_write"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs"
